mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns with a t-statistic above 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive equity returns over the next month when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when macro output gap turns positive, or forward returns are flat during capacity expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation readings trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Elevated monthly CPI prints are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of cases, indicating market pricing of inflation as benign.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent payroll gains and unemployment declines do not yield a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates directly translate into stronger corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and deteriorating business activity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, refuting the supply-weakness transmission channel.
